umm.digiquilt.xmlsaveload.handlers
Class DelegatingHandler

java.lang.Object
  org.xml.sax.helpers.DefaultHandler
      umm.digiquilt.xmlsaveload.handlers.DelegatingHandler
All Implemented Interfaces:
ContentHandler, DTDHandler, EntityResolver, ErrorHandler
Direct Known Subclasses:
BlockHandler, ChallengeFileHandler, ChallengeHandler, DigiQuiltSAXHandler, FractionChallengeHandler, GridHandler, HistoryHandler, LineHandler, PatchHandler, TextHandler
public abstract class DelegatingHandler
extends DefaultHandler

A ContentHandler which allows multiple ContentHandlers to also get SAX events. Normally only one Handler is allowed to receive these.

A subclass can receive XML events from an XML reader by overriding the methods in DefaultHandler and dealing with them. Alternatively, they can create a new DelegatingHandler and pass control off to it temporarily. The child may be specially built to deal with a certain tag in XML. After the child is done, it can pass a resulting Object to its parent using the childFinished() method and return control to the parent by executing stopHandlingEvents().

Version:
$Revision: 1.1 $
Author:
Jason Biatek, last changed by $Author: biatekjt $ on $Date: 2009-06-10 16:58:41 $

Constructor Summary
DelegatingHandler(DelegatingHandler parent)
          Create a child DelegatingHandler.
DelegatingHandler(XMLReader xmlReader)
          Create a top-level DelegatingHandler to receive events from the given XMLReader.
 
Method Summary
abstract  void childFinished(Object o)
          Children of MultiEventHandlers can use this method to pass Objects back up to their parents.
 XMLReader getXMLReader()
           
 void startHandlingEvents()
          Tell this DelegatingHandler to take over receiving events.
 void stopHandlingEvents()
          Relinquish control to the parent.

Constructor Detail

DelegatingHandler

public DelegatingHandler(XMLReader xmlReader)
Create a top-level DelegatingHandler to receive events from the given XMLReader.

Parameters:
xmlReader -

DelegatingHandler

public DelegatingHandler(DelegatingHandler parent)
Create a child DelegatingHandler. The given handler will be its parent.

Parameters:
parent -
Method Detail

getXMLReader

public XMLReader getXMLReader()
Returns:
the XMLReader for this handler.

startHandlingEvents

public void startHandlingEvents()
Tell this DelegatingHandler to take over receiving events. Control should be returned to the parent when this handler is done, probably accompanied with an object being passed up as well.

stopHandlingEvents

public void stopHandlingEvents()
Relinquish control to the parent. The parent handler will resume receiving events from the XMLReader.

childFinished

public abstract void childFinished(Object o)
Children of MultiEventHandlers can use this method to pass Objects back up to their parents. The parent is responsible for remembering what it delegated to so that it knows what to expect.

Parameters:
o - the Object that the child wishes to pass to the parent.
